My work is grounded in the principle that every piece of data can be seen and processed as a meaningful, contextual representation, enabling identification, analyses, and predictions of relations at the macro and micro level. This approach has yielded an expansive impact on AbbVie's entire product cycle. By enabling forecasting and simulation right from early discovery to clinical trial designs, I am confident about the contribution my research is making to bring the next breakthrough therapy to market. While my initial venture into artificial intelligence and natural language processing was rooted in machine translation, I soon realized that its application can be far-reaching. By adapting the sequence-to-sequence model, we can push its limits beyond language boundaries and ‘translate’ different kinds of data sets optimally. One of my proud achievements at AbbVie has been the creation of a proprietary neural machine translation model within the Language Services department. However, recent endeavours include developing a model to predict sales forecasts and anticipate errors in production machines. For me, a highlight has been using predictive models to ‘translate’ compound testing data into predictions for test results of new compounds. It is incredibly exciting to consider how such an approach can accelerate the discovery process. Of course, there is much left to accomplish. As I continue pushing boundaries, reimagining the potential of AI and exploring its limitless possibilities, I remain guided by AbbVie's central mission.
